degraded.reason: Pre-existing sweep-outbox warning from setup_iterate_worktree (trg-671bb0a2: a status append for an id with no append anywhere, dropped by a prior merge) — unrelated to this change; non-fatal, the worktree was still created.

External LLM plan-review (Advisory at medium per Override Classes) deliberately skipped for this surgical, fully-tested consistency refactor; the mandatory internal code-reviewer cascade ran (verdict APPROVE-WITH-NITS) and the cwd->main_root nit was applied.

surface_verification.justification: Pure internal bloat-gate mechanics: aligns the PostToolUse marker writer (check_file_size) to key delta/was_in_allowlist off the worktree's own baseline (mirroring the PR #186 Stop-gate reader fix) + extracts a shared worktree_root_for SSoT. No UI/API/CLI surface is introduced or changed; the marker JSON schema is unchanged. Composition verified empirically by test_integration_writer_reader_compose_on_worktree_only_baseline (real writer + real Stop-gate subprocess) plus full shared (3419) + integration (147) suites green.

behaviors.behavior: AC-1: worktree_root_for reconstructs the owning worktree root for a .worktrees/<slug>/ path and returns None for a main-tree path (backslash-tolerant)
AC-2: writer (check_file_size) records delta=anti-ratchet/was_in_allowlist=true for a file present in the WORKTREE baseline but absent from MAIN's
Writer MAIN fallback preserved when no worktree baseline is present (trg-305e2aab behavior unchanged)
AC-3: reader (bloat_gate_on_stop) uses the shared worktree_root_for; existing Stop-gate + worktree-baseline behavior intact
behaviors.evidence: shared/tests/test_bloat_gate_on_stop.py (17) + test_bloat_gate_worktree_baseline.py (3) + test_bloat_marker_worktree_aware.py reader tests PASSED

behaviors.behavior: AC-4: real writer + real Stop-gate subprocess compose on a worktree-only baseline — at-ceiling does not block, over-ceiling blocks (matches CI anti-ratchet)
